description_long: This pastry shop is a tall, narrow, multi-story building with a steeply pitched gabled roof and a prominent chimney. The facade features a classic half-timbered design with exposed wooden beams and white infill panels, evoking a traditional European or Tudor architectural style. The front of the shop has large glass windows on the ground floor, likely for displaying goods, and several symmetrically arranged windows on the upper floors. The side and rear walls are more plain, with minimal decoration and a mix of plaster and brickwork near the base. The roof is covered in textured, weathered shingles, and the building's overall appearance suggests historical charm and a cozy, inviting atmosphere. The entrance is located at the front, with no visible signage or outdoor seating.
description: A tall, narrow, half-timbered pastry shop with a steep gabled roof, large front windows, and a traditional European facade. The building exudes historical charm and features a mix of wood, plaster, and brick.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, large ground-floor windows, and symmetrical upper windows with half-timbered detailing.
description_view_1: Side view revealing a plain plaster wall with minimal windows and a visible chimney structure extending from the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view with a single door, smaller windows, and continued half-timbered design, but less ornate than the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, with a plain plaster wall and no significant architectural features.
attribute_architectural_style: Tudor Revival (half-timbered) with steep gabled roof and traditional European elements
